cards.title: Mirroring Works
cards.body: Mirroring a person's body language increases the chances of a successful sales pitch by building rapport and trust.

cards.title: Loss Aversion
cards.body: People tend to fear losses more than they value gains, which can be leveraged in sales by framing products as loss prevention.

cards.title: Scarcity Principle
cards.body: Limited availability of a product increases its perceived value, making it more attractive to potential buyers.

cards.title: Social Proof
cards.body: Customer testimonials and reviews can increase sales by providing social proof of a product's quality and effectiveness.

cards.title: Anchoring Effect
cards.body: Providing an initial high price can make subsequent prices seem more reasonable by comparison, increasing the chances of a sale.

cards.title: Reciprocity
cards.body: Offering something for free can create a sense of obligation in the recipient, making them more likely to buy from you in the future.

cards.title: Urgency
cards.body: Creating a sense of urgency can motivate potential buyers to make a decision, increasing the chances of a sale.

cards.title: Foot-in-the-Door Technique
cards.body: Getting someone to agree to a small request can increase the likelihood of them agreeing to a larger request later.

sources.title: The Foot-in-the-Door Technique
sources.publisher: Journal of Personality and Social Psychology

cards.title: Door-in-the-Face Technique
cards.body: Making an extreme request that is likely to be rejected can increase the chances of a subsequent, more moderate request being accepted.

sources.title: The Door-in-the-Face Technique
sources.publisher: Journal of Personality and Social Psychology

cards.title: The Power of Free
cards.body: Offering free products or services can increase sales by creating a sense of reciprocity and building trust with potential customers.

sources.title: Free: The Future of a Radical Price

cards.title: Emotional Connections
cards.body: Creating an emotional connection with potential customers can increase the chances of a sale by making the product more relatable and personal.

cards.title: Storytelling
cards.body: Using storytelling techniques can increase the effectiveness of a sales pitch by making the product more memorable and engaging.

cards.title: Authenticity
cards.body: Being authentic and transparent can increase trust with potential customers, making them more likely to buy from you.

cards.title: Consistency
cards.body: Following up with potential customers and being consistent in your messaging can increase the chances of a sale.

cards.title: Liking
cards.body: People are more likely to buy from someone they like, making it essential to build a rapport with potential customers.

cards.title: Authority
cards.body: Establishing authority and credibility can increase trust with potential customers, making them more likely to buy from you.

cards.title: Commitment
cards.body: Getting someone to commit to a small purchase can increase the likelihood of them making a larger purchase in the future.

sources.title: The Psychology of Commitment
sources.publisher: Journal of Personality and Social Psychology

cards.title: Exclusivity
cards.body: Making a product or service exclusive can increase its perceived value and make it more desirable to potential customers.

cards.title: Nostalgia
cards.body: Using nostalgia in marketing can increase sales by evoking positive emotions and creating a sense of familiarity.

cards.title: Surprise
cards.body: Using surprise in marketing can increase sales by creating a sense of excitement and making the product more memorable.

cards.title: Social Identity
cards.body: People tend to buy from companies that align with their social identity, making it essential to understand your target audience's values.

sources.title: Social Identity Theory
sources.publisher: Journal of Personality and Social Psychology

cards.title: Personalization
cards.body: Personalizing marketing messages can increase sales by making the product more relevant and tailored to the individual customer.

cards.title: The Power of Now
cards.body: Creating a sense of urgency can increase sales by motivating customers to make a decision quickly.

cards.title: Word-of-Mouth
cards.body: Encouraging satisfied customers to share their experiences can increase sales by providing social proof and building credibility.

cards.title: Gamification
cards.body: Using game design elements in marketing can increase sales by making the experience more engaging and interactive.

cards.title: Sustainability
cards.body: Emphasizing the environmental and social benefits of a product can increase sales by appealing to customers' values.

cards.title: Customer Experience
cards.body: Providing a positive customer experience can increase sales by building loyalty and encouraging repeat business.

cards.title: Influencer Marketing
cards.body: Partnering with influencers can increase sales by reaching new audiences and building credibility.

quizzes.question: What is the term for the technique of mirroring a person's body language to build rapport?
quizzes.explanation: Mirroring is a technique used to build rapport and trust by imitating a person's body language.

quizzes.question: What is the term for the concept that people tend to follow the actions of others?
quizzes.explanation: Social proof is the concept that people tend to follow the actions of others, especially when they are uncertain.

quizzes.question: What is the term for the technique of getting someone to agree to a small request to increase the likelihood of them agreeing to a larger request later?
quizzes.explanation: The foot-in-the-door technique involves getting someone to agree to a small request to increase the likelihood of them agreeing to a larger request later.

quizzes.question: What is the term for the technique of making an extreme request that is likely to be rejected to increase the chances of a subsequent, more moderate request being accepted?
quizzes.explanation: The door-in-the-face technique involves making an extreme request that is likely to be rejected to increase the chances of a subsequent, more moderate request being accepted.

quizzes.question: What is the term for the concept that people tend to like and trust those who are similar to themselves?
quizzes.explanation: Liking is the concept that people tend to like and trust those who are similar to themselves, which can increase the chances of a sale.

quizzes.question: What is the term for the concept that people tend to value things more when they are scarce?
quizzes.explanation: Scarcity is the concept that people tend to value things more when they are scarce, which can increase the chances of a sale.

quizzes.question: What is the term for the technique of using storytelling to make a product more memorable and engaging?
quizzes.explanation: Storytelling is the technique of using narratives to make a product more memorable and engaging, which can increase the chances of a sale.
